headline: Prison overcrowding reaches 127% capacity nationwide
context: The Malaysian Prisons Department reported that 55 prisons nationwide operated at 127% capacity in 2025, raising serious concerns about conditions.

cards.big: 55 prisons hold 127% of designed capacity affecting 74,000 inmates
cards.sub: Overcrowding worsens health risks, violence, and rehabilitation prospects

cards.big: Prisons Act 1995 sets minimum standards rarely met in overcrowded facilities
cards.sub: Suhakam inspections found 12 prisons below minimum space requirements

cards.big: UN Mandela Rules require 5.4 sq metres per prisoner minimum
cards.sub: Malaysian average reported at 3.2 sq metres per inmate in 2025

cards.big: Recidivism rate stands at 28% partly due to inadequate rehabilitation programs
cards.sub: Overcrowding diverts resources from education and skills training

cards.big: Warehousing 74,000 people without rehabilitation creates a cycle, not a solution
Government allocated RM180 million for 3 new prisons in Budget 2025
